description:
You are a storage administrator who has been handed three per-server CSV files that report disk-usage for each mount point.  
Your job is to consolidate these files, calculate utilisation percentages, and generate two new artefacts (one CSV and one JSON) as well as a tiny log file that will later be consumed by automated checks.

Initial data location  
• Directory: /home/user/disk_reports  
• Files already present inside that directory:  
  – server_a.csv  
  – server_b.csv  
  – server_c.csv  

Structure of every *input* CSV file  
1st row is the header.  
Columns are exactly: mount_point,total_gb,used_gb  
All numeric fields are integers (gigabytes).  
Example line:  /home,200,150

Your tasks  

STEP 1 – Create /home/user/disk_reports/combined_usage.csv  
a. Merge the three input CSVs into a single file.  
b. Add a new leading column named server that records which server the row came from (values must be server_a, server_b or server_c exactly).  
c. Add a trailing column named used_percent that you calculate as: (used_gb ÷ total_gb × 100) rounded to two decimal places.  
d. The header row for the new file must therefore be:  
   server,mount_point,total_gb,used_gb,used_percent  
e. Preserve original row order *per file* (all rows from server_a first, then server_b, then server_c).  

STEP 2 – Create /home/user/disk_reports/high_usage.json  
a. Parse combined_usage.csv and select every row whose used_percent is strictly greater than 80.00.  
b. Produce a JSON array (pretty-printed, two-space indentation, newline-terminated) where each element is an object having exactly these keys in this order:  
   1. "server" (string)  
   2. "mount_point" (string)  
   3. "used_percent" (number, not a string)  
c. The elements in the array must be sorted by used_percent in descending order (highest utilisation first).  

STEP 3 – Create /home/user/disk_reports/process.log  
A plain-text file containing **exactly** three newline-terminated lines (no extra blank lines) in the format KEY=VALUE:  
COMBINED_ROWS=<total number of data rows in combined_usage.csv>  
HIGH_USAGE_ROWS=<number of elements written to high_usage.json>  
THRESHOLD=80  
All KEY names must be uppercase and appear in the order shown above.  

Constraints that the automatic grader will enforce  
• Do not change the names or locations of any files/directories specified above.  
• combined_usage.csv must contain the header plus every original data row augmented with the two extra columns.  
• Numeric percentages in used_percent must always show exactly two digits after the decimal point (e.g., 75.00, 33.33).  
• high_usage.json must be valid JSON, pretty-printed with two-space indentation, end with a single newline, and contain only the rows whose used_percent > 80.00.  
• process.log must match the three-line, single-newline-terminated format described in STEP 3.  

When you are finished, you should have precisely three new files—combined_usage.csv, high_usage.json and process.log—inside /home/user/disk_reports alongside the original CSV inputs.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts

1. /home/user/disk_reports/server_a.csv  
Contents:  
mount_point,total_gb,used_gb  
/,50,30  
/home,200,150  
/var,100,90  

2. /home/user/disk_reports/server_b.csv  
Contents:  
mount_point,total_gb,used_gb  
/,60,20  
/home,250,240  
/opt,80,30  

3. /home/user/disk_reports/server_c.csv  
Contents:  
mount_point,total_gb,used_gb  
/,40,35  
/data,500,450  
/backup,300,100  

Expected artefacts after correct task completion

A. /home/user/disk_reports/combined_usage.csv  
Exact content (including header):  
server,mount_point,total_gb,used_gb,used_percent  
server_a,/,50,30,60.00  
server_a,/home,200,150,75.00  
server_a,/var,100,90,90.00  
server_b,/,60,20,33.33  
server_b,/home,250,240,96.00  
server_b,/opt,80,30,37.50  
server_c,/,40,35,87.50  
server_c,/data,500,450,90.00  
server_c,/backup,300,100,33.33  

B. /home/user/disk_reports/high_usage.json  
Exact content (note two-space indentation and trailing newline):  
[
  {
    "server": "server_b",
    "mount_point": "/home",
    "used_percent": 96.0
  },
  {
    "server": "server_a",
    "mount_point": "/var",
    "used_percent": 90.0
  },
  {
    "server": "server_c",
    "mount_point": "/data",
    "used_percent": 90.0
  },
  {
    "server": "server_c",
    "mount_point": "/",
    "used_percent": 87.5
  }
]

C. /home/user/disk_reports/process.log  
Exact content (including final newline):  
COMBINED_ROWS=9  
HIGH_USAGE_ROWS=4  
THRESHOLD=80  

All three generated files must be world-readable (chmod 644) and owned by the current user.
